basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: al-Ṭalāq — “Divorce” — orders the parting of spouses with a gentleness and exactness meant to preserve, even in rupture, dignity and faithfulness to the companionship now ending. Qushayrī, without lingering on legal detail, draws from it a pedagogy of the heart: servitude (ʿubūdiyya) is to stand at the prescribed limit (ḥadd), neither falling short of it nor overstepping it. Above all, he makes this sūra the great treatise on godly fear and trust: whoever fears God, God makes for him a way out (makhraj) and provides for him from where he does not expect; whoever relies on God, God suffices him (wa-man yatawakkal ʿalā llāhi fa-huwa ḥasbuh). He unfolds here a subtle doctrine of tawakkul: not to demand to know when, how, and by whose hand the matter will resolve — for such knowledge is among those one should seek refuge from — but to abide in silence and contentment until the hour of unveiling. The sūra closes on the seven heavens and their earthly likenesses, traversed by the descending Command, that one may know God encompasses all things in His knowledge.
themes: the way out granted by godly fear
tawakkul and the sufficiency of God
provision from where one does not expect

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِذَا طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّسَاءَ فَطَلِّقُوهُنَّ لِعِدَّتِهِنَّ وَأَحْصُوا الْعِدَّةَ ۖ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ رَبَّكُمْ ۖ لَا تُخْرِجُوهُنَّ مِن بُيُوتِهِنَّ وَلَا يَخْرُجْنَ إِلَّا أَن يَأْتِينَ بِفَاحِشَةٍ مُّبَيِّنَةٍ ۚ وَتِلْكَ حُدُودُ اللَّهِ ۚ وَمَن يَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَ اللَّهِ فَقَدْ ظَلَمَ نَفْسَهُ ۚ لَا تَدْرِي لَعَلَّ اللَّهَ يُحْدِثُ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ أَمْرًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nabiyyu idhā ṭallaqtumu n-nisāʾa fa-ṭalliqūhunna li-ʿiddatihinna wa-aḥṣū l-ʿiddata wa-ttaqū llāha rabbakum lā tukhrijūhunna min buyūtihinna wa-lā yakhrujna illā an yaʾtīna bi-fāḥishatin mubayyinatin wa-tilka ḥudūdu llāhi wa-man yataʿadda ḥudūda llāhi fa-qad ẓalama nafsahu lā tadrī laʿalla llāha yuḥdithu baʿda dhālika amran
versets.trad: O Prophet! When you divorce women, do so at the conclusion of their waiting period, and count the period carefully. Fear God, your Lord! Do not turn them out of their homes, nor let them leave, unless they commit a manifest indecency. Such are the limits set by God. Whoever transgresses God's prescriptions wrongs his own soul. You do not know: it may be that God will, thereafter, bring about something new.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: divorce, though it be separation, God did not make forbidden — even if, in one regard, it remains hateful. He turns “the limits of God” (ḥudūd Allāh) into a definition of servitude: worship is to stand at the limit, neither diminishing nor exceeding it; and whoever observes his limit with God, God makes pure his covenant. As for “perhaps God will bring about something new,” he reads in it the possible turning of hearts: hearts turn over — a regret, a return, a new inclination; none controls tomorrow.

mots.racine: ط ل ق / ن س و
mots.def: ṭallaqa: to divorce, repudiate; nisāʾ: women.

mots.racine: ط ل ق / ع د د
mots.sens: divorce them at their waiting period

mots.racine: ح د د
mots.sens: such are the limits of God

mots.ar: لَعَلَّ اللَّهَ يُحْدِثُ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ أَمْرًا
mots.tr: laʿalla llāha yuḥdithu baʿda dhālika amran
mots.racine: ح د ث / أ م ر
mots.sens: it may be God will bring about something after that
mots.def: aḥdatha: to bring forth, originate; amr: matter, thing.
mots.gram: particle of hope + verb (form IV) + object

versets.ar: فَإِذَا بَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَّ فَأَمْسِكُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ فَارِقُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ وَأَشْهِدُوا ذَوَيْ عَدْلٍ مِّنكُمْ وَأَقِيمُوا الشَّهَادَةَ لِلَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ يُوعَظُ بِهِ مَن كَانَ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُ مَخْرَجًا
versets.tr: fa-idhā balaghna ajalahunna fa-amsikūhunna bi-maʿrūfin aw fāriqūhunna bi-maʿrūfin wa-ashhidū dhaway ʿadlin minkum wa-aqīmū sh-shahādata lillāhi dhālikum yūʿaẓu bihi man kāna yuʾminu billāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri wa-man yattaqi llāha yajʿal lahu makhrajan
versets.trad: When they reach their term, keep them honourably or part from them honourably. Call two just witnesses from among you, and bear witness before God. To this is exhorted whoever believes in God and the Last Day. And whoever fears God, God will make for him a way out,
themes: the way out of godly fear
versets.commentaire: “Honourably” (bi-maʿrūf) returns twice: whether one keeps or leaves, let it always be in beauty of conduct. Then comes the central pearl of the sūra: wa-man yattaqi llāha yajʿal lahu makhrajan — whoever fears God, God grants him a way out. Qushayrī: when the servant is sincere in his fear, God draws him out of his entanglements as a hair is drawn from dough — nothing clinging to it; He raises over the godfearing the pavilions of His care, takes him under the wing of His protection, turns cares away from his heart, and strips him of all encumbrance.

mots.racine: م س ك / ع ر ف
mots.def: amsaka: to keep, retain; maʿrūf: what is fitting, good usage.

mots.racine: ف ر ق
ش ه د / ع د ل

mots.ar: وَمَن يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُ مَخْرَجًا
mots.tr: wa-man yattaqi llāha yajʿal lahu makhrajan
mots.racine: و ق ي / خ ر ج
mots.sens: whoever fears God, He grants him a way out

versets.ar: وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ ۚ وَمَن يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللَّهِ فَهُوَ حَسْبُهُ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ بَالِغُ أَمْرِهِ ۚ قَدْ جَعَلَ اللَّهُ لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدْرًا
versets.tr: wa-yarzuqhu min ḥaythu lā yaḥtasibu wa-man yatawakkal ʿalā llāhi fa-huwa ḥasbuhu inna llāha bālighu amrihi qad jaʿala llāhu li-kulli shayʾin qadran
versets.trad: and will provide for him from where he does not reckon. For God suffices whoever relies on Him. God always accomplishes His command. God has set a measure for everything.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dwells on the exact wording: God does not say “whoever relies on Him, his reliance suffices him,” but fa-huwa ḥasbuh — He suffices him; it is God Himself, not the act of trust, that is the sufficiency. And he defines tawakkul: to see yourself outside the power-to-act (munna); the decrees are fulfilled upon you without any contriving of yours — the servant's way is tranquil extinction and contentment, not demanding to know the matter in advance. Since everything has its measured decree (qadr), trust does not change the decree; it changes the heart that bears it — and that is the greatest of blessings.

mots.racine: ر ز ق / ح س ب
mots.sens: He provides for him whence he reckons not
mots.def: razaqa: to provide; iḥtasaba: to reckon, foresee.

mots.ar: وَمَن يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللَّهِ فَهُوَ حَسْبُهُ
mots.tr: wa-man yatawakkal ʿalā llāhi fa-huwa ḥasbuh
mots.racine: و ك ل / ح س ب
mots.sens: whoever relies on God, He suffices him
mots.def: tawakkala: to rely; ḥasb: what suffices.

mots.racine: ب ل غ / أ م ر
mots.def: bāligh: attaining its end; amr: command.

mots.ar: قَدْ جَعَلَ اللَّهُ لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدْرًا
mots.tr: qad jaʿala llāhu li-kulli shayʾin qadran
mots.racine: ج ع ل / ق د ر
mots.sens: God set for everything a measure

versets.ar: وَاللَّائِي يَئِسْنَ مِنَ الْمَحِيضِ مِن نِّسَائِكُمْ إِنِ ارْتَبْتُمْ فَعِدَّتُهُنَّ ثَلَاثَةُ أَشْهُرٍ وَاللَّائِي لَمْ يَحِضْنَ ۚ وَأُولَاتُ الْأَحْمَالِ أَجَلُهُنَّ أَن يَضَعْنَ حَمْلَهُنَّ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُ مِنْ أَمْرِهِ يُسْرًا
versets.tr: wa-llāʾī yaʾisna mina l-maḥīḍi min nisāʾikum ini rtabtum fa-ʿiddatuhunna thalāthatu ashhurin wa-llāʾī lam yaḥiḍna wa-ulātu l-aḥmāli ajaluhunna an yaḍaʿna ḥamlahunna wa-man yattaqi llāha yajʿal lahu min amrihi yusran
versets.trad: For those of your women who no longer expect menstruation, if you are in doubt, their waiting period is three months, and likewise for those who have not yet menstruated. For pregnant women, their term is reached when they deliver their burden. And whoever fears God, God makes his affair easy for him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī takes up here his doctrine of tawakkul: trust is to see yourself outside the power-to-act; the decrees are fulfilled upon you with no plan of yours nor access to their secret. He draws a commentary on the hadith “I seek refuge in You from knowledge that does not profit”: among the useless kinds of knowledge to seek refuge from — wishing to know when this matter will be set right, by what means, and by whose hand: all that is confusion; abide in silence and fair contentment, until the hour of unveiling comes. Hence the clausula: to whoever fears Him, God makes the matter easy (yusr).

mots.racine: ي أ س / ح ي ض
ر ي ب
ح م ل
ج ع ل / ي س ر

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ أَمْرُ اللَّهِ أَنزَلَهُ إِلَيْكُمْ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يُكَفِّرْ عَنْهُ سَيِّئَاتِهِ وَيُعْظِمْ لَهُ أَجْرًا
versets.tr: dhālika amru llāhi anzalahu ilaykum wa-man yattaqi llāha yukaffir ʿanhu sayyiʾātihi wa-yuʿẓim lahu ajran
versets.trad: Such is the command of God, which He has sent down to you. From whoever fears God, God effaces his evil deeds and magnifies his reward.
versets.commentaire: Third occurrence of the promise to whoever fears God (taqwā): after the way out and the ease, here is the effacing of sins and the greatness of reward. Qushayrī sees in this threefold promise an ascending pedagogy: fear frees first from care, then from hardship, then from sin itself — taqwā is no constraint but a path of lightening.

mots.racine: أ م ر
mots.sens: such is the command of God

mots.racine: ن ز ل
mots.sens: He sent it down to you

mots.racine: ك ف ر / س و أ
ع ظ م / أ ج ر
mots.def: aʿẓama: to make immense; ajr: reward.

versets.ar: أَسْكِنُوهُنَّ مِنْ حَيْثُ سَكَنتُم مِّن وُجْدِكُمْ وَلَا تُضَارُّوهُنَّ لِتُضَيِّقُوا عَلَيْهِنَّ ۚ وَإِن كُنَّ أُولَاتِ حَمْلٍ فَأَنفِقُوا عَلَيْهِنَّ حَتَّىٰ يَضَعْنَ حَمْلَهُنَّ ۚ فَإِنْ أَرْضَعْنَ لَكُمْ فَآتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ ۖ وَأْتَمِرُوا بَيْنَكُم بِمَعْرُوفٍ ۖ وَإِن تَعَاسَرْتُمْ فَسَتُرْضِعُ لَهُ أُخْرَىٰ
versets.tr: askinūhunna min ḥaythu sakantum min wujdikum wa-lā tuḍārrūhunna li-tuḍayyiqū ʿalayhinna wa-in kunna ulāti ḥamlin fa-anfiqū ʿalayhinna ḥattā yaḍaʿna ḥamlahunna fa-in arḍaʿna lakum fa-ātūhunna ujūrahunna wa-ʾtamirū baynakum bi-maʿrūfin wa-in taʿāsartum fa-saturḍiʿu lahu ukhrā
versets.trad: Lodge these women where you dwell, according to your means, and do not harm them so as to make their lives hard. If they are pregnant, provide for them until they deliver their burden. If they nurse the child born of you, give them their due payment. Settle matters amicably between you; but if you cannot agree, let another woman nurse the child.
versets.commentaire: The spirit of the sūra: never to pile upon the woman both separation and destitution. Qushayrī: the indication here is not to combine upon them rupture and deprivation, which would double their trial. Even in the defeat of the marital bond, the watchword remains honourable conduct (maʿrūf) and accord: to consult before dividing, to prefer the amicable to the harsh — for the manner of leaving reveals the truth of a heart.

mots.racine: س ك ن
mots.def: askana: to house; sakana: to dwell.

mots.racine: و ج د
ض ر ر / ض ي ق
mots.sens: do not harm them to straiten them
mots.def: ḍārra: to harm; ḍayyaqa: to constrict.

mots.racine: أ م ر / ع ر ف

versets.ar: لِيُنفِقْ ذُو سَعَةٍ مِّن سَعَتِهِ ۖ وَمَن قُدِرَ عَلَيْهِ رِزْقُهُ فَلْيُنفِقْ مِمَّا آتَاهُ اللَّهُ ۚ لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا مَا آتَاهَا ۚ سَيَجْعَلُ اللَّهُ بَعْدَ عُسْرٍ يُسْرًا
versets.tr: li-yunfiq dhū saʿatin min saʿatihi wa-man qudira ʿalayhi rizquhu fa-l-yunfiq mimmā ātāhu llāhu lā yukallifu llāhu nafsan illā mā ātāhā sa-yajʿalu llāhu baʿda ʿusrin yusran
versets.trad: Let the one of ample means spend out of his abundance, and let the one whose means are restricted spend out of what God has given him. God burdens no soul beyond what He has granted it. God will make ease follow hardship.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: according to capacity the servant is asked to give; and whoever has just enough for the moment, then frets about hoarding for tomorrow — his fretting does not please God. On “after hardship, ease” (baʿda ʿusrin yusran), he distinguishes the ranks of men: awaiting ease from God is the state of those of the middle — fallen from the rank of contentment (riḍā), where the presence and absence of the cause are equal, but risen above despair. And he cites his own verses: if fate strikes you with its sorrow, rest by holding it light; soon its cloud will clear, and all its turns will pass.

mots.racine: ن ف ق / و س ع
mots.sens: let the well-off spend of his abundance

mots.racine: ق د ر / ر ز ق
mots.def: qudira: to be straitened, limited; rizq: provision.

mots.ar: لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا مَا آتَاهَا
mots.tr: lā yukallifu llāhu nafsan illā mā ātāhā
mots.racine: ك ل ف / أ ت ي
mots.sens: God burdens a soul only with what He gave it

mots.racine: ع س ر / ي س ر
mots.sens: God will make ease after hardship

versets.ar: وَكَأَيِّن مِّن قَرْيَةٍ عَتَتْ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهَا وَرُسُلِهِ فَحَاسَبْنَاهَا حِسَابًا شَدِيدًا وَعَذَّبْنَاهَا عَذَابًا نُّكْرًا
versets.tr: wa-ka-ayyin min qaryatin ʿatat ʿan amri rabbihā wa-rusulihi fa-ḥāsabnāhā ḥisāban shadīdan wa-ʿadhdhabnāhā ʿadhāban nukran
versets.trad: How many a town rebelled against the command of its Lord and His messengers! We brought it to a severe reckoning and inflicted on it a harsh punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses the moral law into an image: he who sows thorns reaps no roses; he who neglects the right of God (ḥaqq Allāh) is not obeyed in the portion of his own desire. And he adds: whoever dares to break God's command, let him be patient in enduring God's punishment. The rebellion (ʿuluww) of past towns is the mirror of every soul that rises up against the limit it has received.

mots.racine: ق ر ي
ع ت و
ح س ب / ش د د
mots.sens: We called it to a severe reckoning
mots.def: ḥāsaba: to call to account; shadīd: severe.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ن ك ر

versets.ar: فَذَاقَتْ وَبَالَ أَمْرِهَا وَكَانَ عَاقِبَةُ أَمْرِهَا خُسْرًا
versets.tr: fa-dhāqat wabāla amrihā wa-kāna ʿāqibatu amrihā khusran
versets.trad: So it tasted the bitter fruit of its conduct, and the outcome of its conduct was ruin.
versets.commentaire: The verb “to taste” (dhāqa) returns like a seal: the consequence (wabāl) is no external punishment stuck on afterward, but the very taste of the act, ripening into loss (khusr). Qushayrī said it at the prior verse: he who sows thorns reaps no roses. The end (ʿāqiba) of a life is but the savour, perceived at last, of what it sowed.

mots.racine: ذ و ق / و ب ل
mots.sens: it tasted the bitterness of its conduct

mots.racine: ع ق ب / خ س ر
mots.sens: the outcome of its conduct was ruin
mots.def: ʿāqiba: end, outcome; khusr: loss, ruin.

versets.ar: أَعَدَّ اللَّهُ لَهُمْ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا ۖ فَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ يَا أُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ۚ قَدْ أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ إِلَيْكُمْ ذِكْرًا
versets.tr: aʿadda llāhu lahum ʿadhāban shadīdan fa-ttaqū llāha yā ulī l-albābi lladhīna āmanū qad anzala llāhu ilaykum dhikran
versets.trad: God has prepared for them a severe punishment. So fear God, O you of understanding, you who believe! God has indeed sent down to you a Reminder:
versets.commentaire: The call addresses “those of understanding” (ūlū l-albāb) — literally the possessors of kernels (lubb), those who see the heart of things behind the husk. And the Reminder (dhikr) sent down, says Qushayrī, contains the explanation of everything: whoever lights himself by its light is guided; whoever takes refuge in the breadth of its court attains, from the disease of ignorance, his cure.

mots.racine: ع د د / ع ذ ب
mots.sens: God has prepared a severe punishment

mots.racine: ل ب ب
ن ز ل / ذ ك ر
mots.sens: God has sent down to you a Reminder

versets.ar: رَّسُولًا يَتْلُو عَلَيْكُمْ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ مُبَيِّنَاتٍ لِّيُخْرِجَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ مِنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى النُّورِ ۚ وَمَن يُؤْمِن بِاللَّهِ وَيَعْمَلْ صَالِحًا يُدْخِلْهُ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدًا ۖ قَدْ أَحْسَنَ اللَّهُ لَهُ رِزْقًا
versets.tr: rasūlan yatlū ʿalaykum āyāti llāhi mubayyinātin li-yukhrija lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti mina ẓ-ẓulumāti ilā n-nūri wa-man yuʾmin billāhi wa-yaʿmal ṣāliḥan yudkhilhu jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā abadan qad aḥsana llāhu lahu rizqan
versets.trad: a Messenger who recites to you the clear verses of God, to bring out those who believe and do righteous deeds from darkness into light. Whoever believes in God and acts righteously, He will admit him into Gardens beneath which rivers flow; he will dwell therein forever. God will grant him a goodly provision.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dwells on “the goodly provision” (rizq ḥasan): the goodly provision is that which stays at the measure of sufficiency — without lack that paralyzes affairs, without excess that turns one from enjoyment through covetousness. And he transposes it to the soul: so too the provisions of hearts; the fairest is the state that occupies the servant in the moment, without lack that torments him with thirst, nor excess that puts him in peril of errors from which only heavenly support delivers.

mots.racine: ر س ل / ت ل و
mots.sens: a Messenger reciting God's signs to you
mots.def: talā: to recite; āyāt: signs, verses.

mots.racine: ب ي ن
ظ ل م / ن و ر
ح س ن / ر ز ق
mots.sens: God has made for him a goodly provision
mots.def: aḥsana: to make fair and good; rizq: provision.

versets.ar: اللَّهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ سَبْعَ سَمَاوَاتٍ وَمِنَ الْأَرْضِ مِثْلَهُنَّ يَتَنَزَّلُ الْأَمْرُ بَيْنَهُنَّ لِتَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ أَحَاطَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عِلْمًا
versets.tr: allāhu lladhī khalaqa sabʿa samāwātin wa-mina l-arḍi mithlahunna yatanazzalu l-amru baynahunna li-taʿlamū anna llāha ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun wa-anna llāha qad aḥāṭa bi-kulli shayʾin ʿilman
versets.trad: God is He who created seven heavens and, for the earth, the like of them. The Command descends among them, that you may know God is, in truth, powerful over all things and that God encompasses all things in knowledge.
themes: the seven heavens and their likenesses
versets.commentaire: The sūra rises at once, from family law to the architecture of the worlds: seven heavens, their earthly likenesses, and between them the Command (amr) ceaselessly descending. Qushayrī: He created what He created, being just in all He creates and ordains, that we may know the dignity of His majesty and the perfection of His attributes; that He accomplishes in truth what He decrees, and encompasses all things in His knowledge. The whole sūra, from divorce to cosmos, speaks one truth: nothing escapes His measure or His knowing.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / س م و
أ ر ض / م ث ل
mots.sens: and of the earth their like

mots.racine: ن ز ل / أ م ر
mots.def: tanazzala: to descend gradually; amr: command.

mots.racine: ح و ط / ع ل م
mots.sens: He encompasses all things in knowledge
mots.def: aḥāṭa: to encompass, surround; ʿilm: knowledge.
